{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56394","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56394/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56394/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56394/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56394","id":2625297469,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6cetw9","number":56394,"title":"MethodError for `round` on `Dates.Time` types","user":{"login":"adknudson","id":18444524,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE4NDQ0NTI0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/18444524?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson","html_url":"https://github.com/adknudson","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-10-30T20:43:14Z","updated_at":"2024-10-30T20:43:14Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"The method `round` is defined for `Dates.TimeTypes`, but fails when the object is a `Dates.Time` type due to `floor` not being defined for `Dates.Time`.

```julia
julia> using Dates

julia> dt = now(); t = Time(dt);

julia> round(dt, Second(30)) # rounding DateTime works
2024-10-30T13:35:30

julia> round(t, Second(30))
ERROR: MethodError: no method matching floor(::Time, ::Second)
The function `floor` exists, but no method is defined for this combination of argument types.
```

Is there a motivating reason why `floor` is not defined for `Time` types?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56394/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56394/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

It would be good to understand the interaction with `let` here since ideally, it would be good to keep it.

","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/PrecompileTools.jl/issues/17/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/PrecompileTools.jl/issues/17/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/PrecompileTools.jl/issues/comments/2002998571","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/PrecompileTools.jl/issues/17#issuecomment-2002998571","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/PrecompileTools.jl/issues/17","id":2002998571,"node_id":"IC_kwDOJX_OeM53Y1Ur","user":{"login":"adknudson","id":18444524,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE4NDQ0NTI0","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/18444524?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson","html_url":"https://github.com/adknudson","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adknudson/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-03-18T06:07:26Z","updated_at":"2024-03-18T06:07:26Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"Is this issue related to why symbols in the `@setup_workload` block are visible in the package namespace? I.e. if I have the following:

```julia
module MyPackage

using PrecompileTools: @setup_workload, @compile_workload

@setup_workload begin
    # these become visible in the namespace
    some_variable = rand(4, 4)
    b = rand(4)
    temp = :U

    @compile_workload begin
        some_variable * b
        some_func(temp)
    end
end

end
```

The variables `some_variable`, `b`, and `temp` are visible if a user types in `MyPackage.[TAB]`. I could wrap the contents of `@setup_workload` in a let-block and then the setup variables would no longer be in the namespace, but that feels wrong.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/PrecompileTools.jl/issues/comments/2002998571/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
